title: About Project
text:
About Project
AI and Machine Learning for Official Statistics (AIML4OS) is major project aimed at supporting National Statistics Institutes in the use of AI and Machine Learning for the production of official statistics. The project, which includes 15 countries across Europe, will run for four years from April 2024 to March 2028. The overarching goal of the project is to provide a comprehensive set of resources that can be used by staff across the European Statistical System (ESS) to implement AI and machine learning in their organisations. These resources will include guidelines, implementation frameworks, working use cases, sample code and information on technical resources.
The project is developed through 13 Work Packages (WPs), six of these are ‘Supporting Workpackages’, which have operational functions and deliver cross-cutting goals for the project. The remaining seven are ‘Use Case Workpackages’ which explore specific themes within AI/ML that are believed will offer huge opportunities for statistical production in the coming years.



Members of the AIML4OS consortium at the project launch in Wiesbaden, April 2024

title: News
text:
News

Our first newsletter, highlighting the project launch, upcoming datalab presentation, and planned participation at NTTS 2025.
The second newsletter has info on three in-person meetings that took place, as well as more details on individual workpackages.
Our third newsletter was released after the first year of the project. It looked at plans across all of the workpackages, as well as an update following NTTS 2025.
Newsletter number four reports on an in-person meeting of WP12, more details on WP10, as well as a template for training materials.
Our fifth newsletter has details on a deliverable on citizen-in-the-loop, news on text classification, and progress on reconstruction of supply-chain networks.

title: Knowledge Repository
text:
This package aims to provide a coherent set of resources and materials for training and knowledge sharing on this project. Central to this is building up a knowledge repository in the form of a single online platform dedicated to the official statistics community and ensuring materials are freely available. The role of the repository is to make the official statistics community familiar with AI/ML solutions and encourage the community to use these solutions for specific statistical products. Standardized training materials about the use of AI/ML methods will be stored in the platform in many forms, such as notebooks, scripts, presentations, sample databases, and possibly several programming languages such as Python and R. The training materials (in English and national languages) will enable self-studying.
Training will be carried out on the technical aspects of using the platform. In addition, workshops will be held to introduce AI/ML techniques for statistical products. Funathons, which are non-competitive hackathons, will be held to promote the platform and the use of AI/ML in official statistics. Participants of Funathons will use ML methods for practical tasks connected with the everyday activities of Official Statistics.
We will collaborate closely with the other Work Packages. Training materials will range from topics for beginners to state-of-the-art solutions, following best practices and standards. This work package will promote use cases by arranging workshops and other training as required.

title: AI/ML Lab
text:
AI/ML Lab
Work Package 3 provides a data science platform, which allows ESS members to deploy and use state-of-the-art data science services on-demand for prototyping on non-sensitive data.
This platform is based on a Kubernetes cluster and provides the users with a wide range of computational resources, such as GPUs. It comes with an extensive catalogue of data science services. The user interface is provided by an open source software, Onyxia, whose development was initiated and carried out throughout by Insee, the French National Statistical Institute. Onyxia’s principles are based on an agnostic vision of data science solutions, leaving room for an easy addition of new software when deemed relevant for the catalogue of services.
The platform can be accessed via this link: https://datalab.sspcloud.fr/
Work Package 3 also provides support for users of the platform and ESS members which wish to deploy Onyxia for their own use.

text:
The Central Statistics Office (CSO), Ireland, are responsible for management and coordination of the AIML4OS project.



AIML4OS on CSO Website

title: AI and Machine Learning for Official Statistics - AIML4OS
text:
AI and Machine Learning for Official Statistics (AIML4OS) is an ESSnet project aimed at delivering a comprehensive suite of resources, guidelines, methodologies and use cases for the implementation of AI/ML in Official Statistics. There are 15 funded countries in the consortium. The project is running from April 2024 to March 2028.

Project Structure
The AIML4OS project is divided into 13 workpackages. Six of these are ‘supporting’ workpackages that provide cross-cutting insight and resources. The remaining seven are ‘use case’ workpackages, each of which explores a specific domain within AI/ML and its relevance to official statistics.

Supporting Workpackages


  


Project Management & Coordination


Support, coordinate and facilitate the consortium activities




  


Communication


Sharing ideas and results from the project, and building communities around AI/ML




  


AI/ML Lab


Deliver a sandpit for AI/ML experimentation and development




  


Ecosystem Monitoring


Gather evidence of the current state of AI/ML use, and identify needs




  


Standards, Methodology & Implementation


Develop guidelines, achieve standardization, and support practical implementation




  


Knowledge Repository


Support integration and maintenance of AI/ML-based solutions through specific training resources




Use Case Workpackages


  


Earth Observation Data


Unlocking potential of earth observation data using AI models




  


Editing


Improving the quality of official statistics through the use of AI/ML in data editing




  


Imputation


Develop, test, and implement AI/ML-based solutions for imputation processes




  


Text to Code


Experiences and potential of the use of AI/ML for classifying and coding




  


Supply Chain Networks


Developing model-based approaches for deriving firm-level network datasets




  


LLMs


Explore and implement LLM technologies to enhance data management, quality control, and process automation




  


Synthetic Data


Generation of synthetic data in official statistics: techniques and applications

title: Communication
text:
Communication
The WP2 aims to share the achievements made to date and underscore the untapped potential in the field of AI/ML for Official Statistics. WP2 focuses on valorizing results attained by National Statistical Institutes (NSIs) through AI/ML techniques, with the aim of disseminating this knowledge to the statistical community and external stakeholders. This WP will communicate a comprehensive overview of available solutions, elevating awareness about AI/ML’s capacity to enhance official statistics and meet stakeholders’ information requirements.
Another crucial objective of WP2 is to foster and reinforce interdependencies among all work packages, as shown in the figure below, and amplify the outcomes of each. Achieving this necessitates enhancing internal communication levels and consolidating all AI/ML-related results to cultivate an active community on these matters.
The planned activities are multifaceted and aimed at promoting collaboration, knowledge sharing, and community interaction within the AI/ML domain. One key strategy is using this open platform to facilitate interaction and the exchange of knowledge and information among stakeholders. Additionally, WP2 will engage with European and international stakeholders active in the AI/ML space, fostering partnerships and leveraging expertise. Regular events, including internal workshops, participation in conferences, funathons, webinars and other collaborative activities, will be organised to facilitate fruitful exchanges between parties. Dissemination events will be strategically planned in conjunction with the release of different WPs to maximize impact and outreach.
Furthermore, WP2 will explore the role of relevant stakeholders in improving AI/ML models, in line with citizen science approaches.
In essence, WP2 serves as the linchpin for communication, collaboration, and knowledge dissemination within the project, playing a pivotal role in maximizing the impact and benefits of AI/ML advancements for the statistical community and stakeholders.

title: Standards, Methodology & Implementation
Earth Observation Data
text:
Earth Observation Data
Recent work from European member states and other institutes show promising results in predicting land use, land cover, land use change, crop yield, crop typology and greenery in cities based on either earth observation data (like satellite and aerial imagery). Using state of the art methods and tools, various AI/ML models were built. The question is whether these kind of developments can be shared, so that more NSI’s can benefit from these developments: build once, run anywhere!
The research question we would like to answer in this work package is: can these existing AI/ML models using earth observation data be generalised over space (countries) and time (periods) and under what conditions?
We want to apply those methods on other countries or other timeframes and compare the results. It would demonstrate what methods and tools to-date seem to work well and what methods lack in useful results, what data sources are needed (and on what level they are available), what kind of (pre)processing is needed, what sorts of infrastructure this (pre)processing requires and how to interpret the results. It also demonstrates if (at all) a generalized processing pipeline and infrastructure is possible and what the specifications of such a pipeline would be. If it proves to be feasible, we will develop methodological and implementation guidelines. And as a side-effect, we will have results in terms of (validated) predictions for other countries/timeframes than the original study.

title: Imputation
text:
Imputation
The main objectives in this work package are to develop, test, and, if shown successful, implement AI/ML-based solutions for imputation processes. We propose three tasks.
Task 9.1 Methodological developments: the goal is to identify and develop methodological proposals aligned and integrated in the statistical production process, which will be tested with specific datasets, populations, and variables. In this first task we include all the preliminary work such as literature review, assessment of methods and proposals to approach each problem, etc. Next, this task also entails the execution and test of the identified solutions for each problem. The work will be devoloped following this research tracks: Early imputation, post-collection imputation, imputation beyond the sample.
Task 9.2 Development of PoC/MVP/prototypes and preparation for deployment in production: if proved successful, the proposals will be promoted to proofs of concept (PoC), minimum viable products (MVP), or prototypes so that feedback can be easily distilled for WP5 on standards, methodological and implementation frameworks. The last goal is to design and implement a CD/CI modular process as close as possible to production conditions to identify the needs for infrastructure, thus serving as input for the ESS AI/ML Lab (WP3).
Task 9.3 Quality aspects: The impact of this use case on new statistical products is clear in the form of possible new experimental statistics but also in methodology terms in order to improve the quality of the process in the current statistical products in relation to timeliness, granularity and accuracy. Quality will be taken into consideration all along the development of the use case.
The duration of the whole work package is estimated in 48 months and with 12 countries participants: Spain (Work Package Leader), Germany, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Slovenia, Italy, Austria, Luxemburg, Denmark, Cyprus, Ireland (Coordinator of the project).
